message:
fix(abi): translate managed mutexattr to host format before host delegation

Root cause: our pthread_mutexattr_t uses a custom bitfield encoding
(valid bit at bit 6, protocol at bits 2-3) that differs from glibc's
internal layout. When pthread_mutex_init delegated to host glibc with
our encoded attr, glibc misinterpreted the valid bit as part of the
mutex kind, creating a PTHREAD_PRIO_PROTECT mutex with invalid
priority. This triggered the __pthread_tpp_change_priority assertion
on any subsequent lock/unlock.

Fix: when delegating pthread_mutex_init to host and the attr has our
valid bit set, create a fresh glibc-format attr via
pthread_mutexattr_init + pthread_mutexattr_settype, then pass that
to the host.

Fixes: c11threads_abi_test abort (36 tests now pass, was 2/36)
Fixes: pthread_abi_test clockwait abort path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
